title: nepeta azurea
intro: Nepeta azurea, also known as azure catmint or azure nepeta, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its blue to purple flowers and its ability to attract pollinators. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as sore throats, colds, and skin irritations. The leaves and flowers of the Nepeta azurea plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering a natural remedy for various health benefits, including reduced stress, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
